content.problemStatement:
Chef is trying to open the oven in his kitchen to cook some cookies. However, the oven is special ― you need a password to open it.

Let's define a function f(x)f(x)f(x) for a positive integer xxx as follows:


Consider the decimal representation of xxx (without leading zeros). Split it into the minimum number of contiguous subsequences of digits such that in each subsequence, all digits are identical.
For each subsequence, look at its position in the original decimal representation of xxx. Let's say that the most significant digit it contained was the eee-th digit, where e=0e=0e=0 corresponds to the least significant digit of xxx. For example, 388,822,442388,822,442388,822,442 can be split into subsequences "3", "888", "22", "44", "2", where e=7e=7e=7 for the sequence "888" and e=4e=4e=4 for the sequence "22".
The value of a subsequence which contains a digit ddd repeated one or more times is d⋅10ed \cdot 10^ed⋅10e.
f(x)f(x)f(x) is the sum of values of these subsequences. For example, f(388,822,442)=3⋅108+8⋅107+2⋅104+4⋅102+2⋅100f(388,822,442) = 3 \cdot 10^8 + 8 \cdot 10^7 + 2 \cdot 10^4 + 4 \cdot 10^2 + 2 \cdot 10^0f(388,822,442)=3⋅108+8⋅107+2⋅104+4⋅102+2⋅100.
The oven has a screen which displays two long integers LLL and RRR (with NLN_LNL​ and NRN_RNR​ digits respectively, without leading zeros). The password is the sum of f(x)f(x)f(x) for each xxx between LLL and RRR inclusive.

Chef does not have a lot of time, so he asks for your help. Please find the password for the oven so that he can start cooking as fast as possible. Since this number could be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: LLL and RRR do not contain leading zeros
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NLN_LNL​ and LLL.
The second line contains two space-separated integers NRN_RNR​ and RRR.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the password.
